feedback_section.description: Open-ended feedback questions about the learning experience. No correct/wrong answer. Used for pedagogical reflection, NOT for AI-CFT assessment. Likert attitude items (L10_1 through L10_8) also in this category.
feedback_section.ai_cft_relevance: Secondary — metacognitive awareness signals. Not scored for correctness.

cognitive_section.description: Cognitively assessed items. These are the primary AI-CFT signals in WS11.
cognitive_section.ai_cft_relevance: High — directly measures AI concept understanding.

WS11_B8a.item_label: Q8 — Classify Strawberry Using the Tree
WS11_B8a.reasoning: Strawberry: Fat=0.3g (≤7.5), Sugar=5.0g (<12.5). Both conditions for 'recommended' are met.

WS11_B8b.item_label: Q8 — Formulate the Decision Rule for Strawberry

credit_rules.full: Rule includes both conditions (Fat threshold AND Sugar threshold) with correct operators and the correct classification outcome.
credit_rules.partial: Rule includes only one condition, OR has correct structure but wrong threshold values.
credit_rules.zero: Empty or rule contradicts the answer in B8a.

WS11_B9.item_label: Q9 — What is Data and Why Do We Need It?

credit_rules.full: Defines data as information/measurements AND explains that data is needed for the DT to learn (train) from. Both components required.
credit_rules.partial: Only defines data without explaining its role in DT, OR only explains role without defining.
credit_rules.zero: Empty or defines a different concept (e.g. gives a DT definition instead of data definition).

WS11_B9.extraction_flag: Sample student B9 contains a DT definition ('Karar ağacı, verideki örüntüleri öğrenerek...'), not a data definition. This indicates Q9 was misextracted — the student's actual Q9 answer was not captured.

WS11_Q10.item_label: Q10 — What Can a Decision Tree Do? (Multiple Select)

option_texts.1: Bir özelliğin hangi niteliğe sahip olduğunu tahmin etmek (Predict a feature's category) → CORRECT
option_texts.2: Yeni nesneler için kararlar vermek (Make decisions for new objects) → CORRECT
option_texts.3: İyi bir yemek planı oluşturmak (Create a meal plan) → WRONG
option_texts.4: Gerçekliğin bir parçasını modellemek (Model part of reality) → CORRECT
option_texts.5: Bir gıdanın ne kadar önerilebilir olduğunu belirlemek (Determine recommendability) → CORRECT
option_texts.6: Bir nesnenin hangi özelliklere sahip olduğunu tahmin etmek (Predict features of an object) → WRONG
option_texts.7: Birine ne yemesi gerektiğini söylemek (Tell someone what to eat) → WRONG
option_texts.8: Alınan kararların ne kadar iyi olduğunu anlamak (Understand how good decisions are) → CORRECT

credit_rules.full: Selects exactly options 1,2,4,5,8 as correct and 3,6,7 as wrong (or equivalent — all 5 correct options marked correctly, all 3 wrong options marked correctly).
credit_rules.partial: Correctly identifies at least 4 of 5 correct options AND correctly identifies at least 2 of 3 wrong options.
credit_rules.zero: Fewer than 4 correct options, or marks a wrong option as correct.

WS11_Q10.extraction_note: Sample student JSON uses WS11_L10_1 through WS11_L10_8 as Likert-scale numbers (4,5,4,3,5,4,4,5). These are attitude scale responses, NOT Q10 multi-select answers. Q10 multi-select was not captured. Flag as Q10_not_captured.

WS11_Q11.item_label: Q11 — Order the Steps for Building a Decision Rule (1-4)

correct_order.step_2: Verileri seçilen özelliğe göre boyuta göre düzenliyorum (Arrange data by feature)
correct_order.step_3: Birçok kartın doğru sınıflandırıldığı iyi bir eşik buldum (Find a threshold with few misclassifications)

credit_rules.full: All 4 steps in correct order: 1=feature selection, 2=data arrangement, 3=threshold finding, 4=decision.
credit_rules.partial: 3 of 4 steps correctly ordered, or all 4 present but 2 adjacent steps swapped.

WS11_Q11.extraction_note: Sample student WS11_L11_1=4, L11_2=5, L11_3=4. These appear to be Likert scores for a sub-scale, NOT the step ordering answers. Q11 ordering was not properly captured. Flag as Q11_not_captured.

WS11_Q12.item_label: Q12 — Why is a Decision Tree Considered AI? (Multiple Select)

option_texts.1: Çünkü bir bilgisayar karar ağaçları oluşturabilir (A computer can build DTs) → CORRECT
option_texts.2: Çünkü bir bilgisayar doğru düşünür (Computer thinks correctly) → WRONG
option_texts.3: Çünkü bir bilgisayar bir insan kadar zekidir (Computer as smart as human) → WRONG
option_texts.4: Çünkü bir karar ağacı ile asla hata yapmazsınız (DT never makes mistakes) → WRONG
option_texts.5: Çünkü otomatik olarak karar vermenizi sağlar (Enables automatic decision-making) → CORRECT

credit_rules.full: Selects 1 and 5 as correct; marks 2,3,4 as wrong.
credit_rules.partial: Selects both 1 and 5 but also selects one wrong option, OR selects only one correct option with all wrong options correctly identified.
credit_rules.zero: Does not select both correct options, or selects multiple wrong options.

WS11_Q12.extraction_note: Sample student JSON does not contain WS11_Q12 at all. This was not extracted. Flag as Q12_not_captured.

WS11_L10_attitude.description: 8-item attitude scale about the DT learning activity. Scores 1-5. Not scored for correctness. Used for engagement/attitude analysis only.

WS11_L11_attitude.description: Secondary attitude sub-scale. Not for AI-CFT scoring.

WS11_demographics.description: Gender, age, teaching subject, years of experience, prior programming. Not for AI-CFT scoring.

critical_missing_items: WS11_Q10 (multi-select: what can DT do) — not captured, extracted as Likert scores instead
WS11_Q11 (step ordering) — not captured, extracted as Likert scores instead
WS11_Q12 (why is DT AI) — completely absent from extraction
WS11_B9 (what is data) — extracted as DT definition instead of data definition

overall_scoring.full: B8a+B8b correct AND Q10 all options correctly identified AND Q11 all 4 steps correctly ordered AND Q12 both correct options identified.
overall_scoring.partial: B8a correct but B8b missing. OR Q10/Q11/Q12 not captured — cannot assign full.
overall_scoring.zero: B8a wrong, or no cognitive items captured.
